justifications.ip1: All three ACs have distinct, specific failing test scenarios: T1/T2 cover AC1 (Slack to '#aml-alerts' with alertId and amount), T3/T4 cover AC2 (email to 'compliance@enterprise.com' with subject 'AML Threshold Breach — ALERT_001'), and T5/T6 cover AC3 (partial failure returns '{slack: "failed", email: "sent"}' and never throws).
justifications.ip2: The plan creates only the two files named in the DoR ('src/compliance/alert-router.js' and 'tests/compliance/alert-router.test.js' plus an optional factory), explicitly states 'No SAR filing, MLRO escalation, deduplication, or threshold detection', and all implementation stays within the Contract Proposal boundary.
justifications.ip3: Every test and implementation section names specific files ('tests/compliance/alert-router.test.js', 'src/compliance/alert-router.js'), specific functions ('createAlertRouter', 'routeAlert(payload)'), and concrete assertions (e.g. 'callArgs.channel === "#aml-alerts"', 'callArgs.subject === "AML Threshold Breach — ALERT_001"').
justifications.ip4: The plan declares all tests are written first in a RED phase before any implementation code, and test-first order is consistently preserved throughout, but there is no explicit 'run npm test to confirm RED' step between the test definitions and the implementation section.
justifications.ip5: NFR-1 (audit logging) has dedicated failing tests T7 and T8 asserting 'auditLogger.log must be called exactly once per routeAlert' with alertId and channel statuses, and the architecture constraint ('MUST use src/audit/audit-logger.js — NOT console.log') is enforced in the implementation and called out in the 'Regulatory & Architecture Compliance' section.

notes: Exceptionally detailed and executable plan with comprehensive test coverage across all ACs and NFR-1; the only minor gap is the absence of an explicit 'run tests to confirm RED' step between test authoring and implementation.
